Tests the behavior of negating negative infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the logical NOT operator when applied to negative infinity in JavaScript. The expression `!(-Infinity)` evaluates to `false`, and the assertion verifies that this evaluation is correct by comparing it to `false`. This test ensures that the logical negation of negative infinity behaves as expected in JavaScript, which is important for validating the consistency of logical operations in the language. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type coercion and logical operations.
